evals.prompt: run a smoke test on maestro, I want to make sure everything works after the refactor I just did
evals.expected_output: Runs all 30 maestro MCP tools in pipeline order, produces pass/fail report with [ok]/[!!]/[--] markers for each step

assertions.description: Report covers all 6 phases: Meta Tools, Feature+Memory, Plan Lifecycle, Task Lifecycle, Promotion+Completion, Conditional Tools
Report uses [ok], [!!], or [--] markers for each step result
Report shows task_block and task_unblock transitions, not just claim/done
Second task runs only after first task is completed, demonstrating dependency ordering
Report mentions graph, handoff, or search tools (even if skipped as unavailable)
Report ends with a summary count line (N passed, N failed, N skipped)
The plan/task references a .md file for the smoke artifact, not .txt

evals.prompt: test all the maestro tools end to end -- I need to verify the pipeline is working before I ship this
evals.expected_output: Creates pipeline-smoke-test feature, exercises feature/plan/task/memory/meta/graph/handoff/search tool groups, produces summary report

assertions.description: Report covers all 6 phases: Meta Tools, Feature+Memory, Plan Lifecycle, Task Lifecycle, Promotion+Completion, Conditional Tools
Report uses [ok], [!!], or [--] markers for each step result

The plan/task references a .md file for the smoke artifact, not .txt
